description: A 'wireki' is a conditional clinical claim about the expected course or outcome of a health condition. It rests on current evidence, assumptions, a time horizon, and the care actually available. Evidence from a population may inform one person's prognosis without dictating their future.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move the tongue forward and unround into high-front 'i'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip and continue into stressed true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: A count picks out one prognosis. Bare 'wireki' discusses prognosis as part of care. Its condition and time horizon set the question; evidence and assumptions support the answer. Alternatives and uncertainty give the claim its bounds. 'kithero' (diagnose) concerns the present clinical conclusion instead of this expected course.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A prognosis describes conditioned possibilities rather than a fixed self or certain future.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Stated assumptions and uncertainty keep a population estimate from becoming destiny for one person.

semantic_domains.cognition: a conditional inference about a health course
semantic_domains.temporal: an expected course or outcome over time
